summary: Publish successful project-render status only after the persistent cache-write attempt settles.

decisions.topic: Successful render status and cache durability ordering
decisions.decision: Keep successful geometry presentation responsive, but do not publish the user-facing successful render state until the configured cache persistence attempt has settled. Cache failure remains non-fatal, and job, snapshot, and generation guards continue to prevent stale completion from replacing newer state.
decisions.rationale: Exact clean Windows Sandbox evidence at 6f68bd4 showed that the harness observed a successful full render and then restarted while the persistent full-quality cache record was still unavailable. The runtime previously published success before awaiting renderCache.put, allowing process shutdown to interrupt a write that the visible status implied was complete.

testEvidence.criterion: A project render must remain in rendering state until its persistent cache-write attempt settles.
testEvidence.test: Hold the disk-cache write promise open, observe runtime state, release the write, and then observe successful full-quality completion.

red.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/application/runtime/workbench-runtime.test.ts -t "does not publish a successful project render before its disk-cache write settles" --reporter=dot
red.outcome: RED: 1/1 failed because runtime state was already success while the disk write remained pending.

green.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/application/runtime/workbench-runtime.test.ts -t "does not publish a successful project render before its disk-cache write settles" --reporter=dot
green.outcome: GREEN: 1/1 passed; state remained rendering until the write was released and then became success.

testEvidence.criterion: The durability ordering must preserve early viewer presentation and the focused M4/runtime and TypeScript contracts.
testEvidence.test: Run the runtime and packaged M4 suites once after reconciling the early-viewer contract, then run TypeScript no-emit.

red.outcome: RED: 1/53 failed because the first implementation delayed viewer presentation behind optional cache I/O.

green.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/application/runtime/workbench-runtime.test.ts tests/scripts/m4-packaged-walkthrough.test.ts --reporter=dot; pnpm.cmd run typecheck
green.outcome: GREEN: 53/53 focused tests passed and TypeScript no-emit passed after retaining early viewer presentation while delaying only successful render status.

nearMisses.sought: Exact clean packaged native M4 cold-start cache evidence
nearMisses.confirmation: Full render was clicked and launched exactly one new engine run, but after restart it completed noncached: clickStarted=true, previewBadge=false, consoleRunsBefore=2, consoleRunsAfter=3. Sandbox cleanup passed.

questions: No owner decision is required; this closes a runtime durability race exposed by exact clean evidence.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
